description_long: This house features a multi-level structure built into a sloped terrain, with a prominent terracotta-tiled gable roof and several overhanging roof sections. The exterior walls are primarily white, with a combination of solid and open balcony spaces, and a large green lawn area on one side. The house includes multiple staircases, both exterior and interior, connecting the different levels and providing access to the main living areas. Large windows and open terraces are visible, allowing for ample natural light and outdoor views. The property is enclosed by a perimeter wall and fencing, with a garage entrance at the lower level. The architectural style blends traditional Mediterranean elements with modern, angular forms.
description: A multi-story house with terracotta gable roofs, white exterior walls, open balconies, and a green lawn, built into a sloped site with multiple staircases and a garage.
description_view_0: Oblique view showing the main facade, terracotta roofs, green lawn, balconies, and staircases leading up the slope.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, continuous gable roof and the solid perimeter wall, with minimal window visibility.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the angular, stepped terrain integration and the back of the house with fewer openings.
description_view_3: Top-down side view showing the roof layout, terraces, and the relationship between the house and the sloped ground.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large windows and open terraces
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a set of exterior steps, a covered porch with overhanging roof, and a prominent door, integrated into the sloped terrain.
